The five classic defects (the “pentalogy”)

1. A defect of the upper abdominal wall (often an omphalocele).

2. A defect of the lower portion of the sternum.

3. A defect of the anterior diaphragm.

4. A defect of the pericardium (the membrane surrounding the heart).

5. Cardiac malformations, {{PERSON}} include ectopia cordis.

DID YOU KNOW THERE IS A MEXICAN BOY WITH A UNIQUE EYE COLOR IN THE WORLD?

During one of his trips through Chiapas, {{PERSON}} a young resident of an indigenous reserve who had a unique eye color, which, along with violet, is considered one of the rarest eye colors in the world.

The child, originally from {{LOCATION}}, has an iris displaying three colors—black, gray, and light blue—a combination that is not only striking but also extremely rare to see.

THE RAREST EYE COLORS IN THE WORLD

The combination of pigments in the iris determines most of the eye colors we are accustomed to seeing.

Brown is the most common eye color, found in more than 50% of the world's population. However, some people have yellow, violet, or even red eyes.

These “rare” eye colors can be grouped into five categories:

• Green. Although relatively well known, green is the fifth rarest eye color in humans. Only about 2% of the world's population has green eyes.

• {{PERSON}}. The fourth rarest eye color is this yellowish-golden shade, {{PERSON}} results from a predominance of lipochrome pigment in the iris.

• Violet. Violet eyes result from a combination of reddish tones and blue reflections. They are most commonly seen in people with albinism, {{LOCATION}}.

• Black. Rarer than violet eyes, true black eyes should not be confused with very dark brown eyes.

• In genuine black eyes—which are said to occur in less than 1% of the population—it is almost impossible to distinguish the iris from the pupil.

• The rarest of all is red eyes, which apparently do exist naturally and are not merely a photographic flash effect.

• As with violet eyes, red eyes are most often associated with albinism and occur in people who completely lack melanin.

I believe fitness industry peeps are the best entrepreneurs and mog all the larpers 

{{PERSON}}, {{PERSON}}, {{PERSON}}, {{PERSON}}, {{PERSON}}, {{PERSON}}, the list goes on endlessly 

If you can’t stay in shape and claim to make bank, I’m just not buying it in most cases 

Not because a six pack magically make you rich

But because getting in shape requires a lot of the same traits that build a business

Discipline

Consistency

Delayed gratification

The ability to do boring things for years without immediate reward

The fitness industry is one of the few industries where you can’t completely fake it

You can rent a {{PERSON}}

You can flex revenue screenshots (many such cases)

But it’s very difficult to fake a decade of training

When you enter the fitness scene, you almost automatically become an entrepreneur

You learn marketing because nobody sees your content otherwise

You learn sales because nobody buys your coaching otherwise

You learn branding because there are a thousand people doing the same thing

You learn leadership because clients need guidance

The average fitness coach is creating content, running sales, {{LOCATION}}, managing clients, building systems and trying to stay in shape at the same time

Most people struggle with one of those

Fitness entrepreneurs are juggling all of them

Most couldn’t survive six months doing it

And if someone genuinely has an incredible physique, a great family life, a successful business and has managed to maintain all of it for years…

You’re a beast (me)

I enjoy various styles of exercise 

But nothing beats training for hypertrophy + getting a pump {{URL}}

Almost 1 year of being a father  

Here’s a few things I’ve learned 

Your relationship with your wife is beyond important, put her first and make her life as easy as possible

Because your child grows up in the world you create together, the better your relationship is, the better everything else tends to be 

Spending time with your daughter or son is the most productive thing you can do 

You will have less time for work and life in general but you will learn to be better and more efficient with the time you do have 

When you become a father, you have this constant proud feeling, you are building your family, you feel you’re on the right path 

You care less about status games and impressing others, you just want your wife and kids to think you’re cool so you prioritise them and stop chasing others, ironically by doing this other people will think you’re cooler 

From a fitness perspective, a lot of dads talk about fatherhood as if it’s the reason they got out of shape.

Sounds harsh, but in reality, most weren’t in great shape beforehand and they use fatherhood as an excuse, not good to blame family life, if anything it should motivate you 

Your kids are watching, your wife is watching

And one day your son or daughter will decide what normal looks like by looking at you, so set the standard 

Being a {{PERSON}}, healthy dad who decides his own schedule is the ultimate flex in life

Quienes te conocemos sabemos que este nombramiento no es fruto de la casualidad, sino de muchos años de trabajo, dedicación y compromiso con nuestro fútbol y con nuestra tierra. Hoy, viendo a {{PERSON}} jugar, viví un instante que me removió por dentro.
Una imagen que me llevó de vuelta a mi etapa de árbitro en las categorías base, cuando pitábamos a los más pequeños y la figura del árbitro se mezclaba con la del monitor.

En aquellos campos aprendías que no solo era cuestión de aplicar el reglamento:
era enseñar, acompañar, corregir y transmitir el espíritu del fútbol desde el corazón.

Hoy vi justo eso:
un niño sacando mal de banda… y un árbitro que, lejos de sancionar, decidió educar.
Le pidió que repitiera el saque y le explicó cómo debía hacerlo.
Un gesto sencillo, pero enorme.
